what_it_does: Documents the architecture and authoring conventions for Counterpart Studio, a React (inline-JSX + Babel) prototype of a Vi-led design and build studio. It defines the global registry-based single sources of truth, rules against hardcoded values, per-folder AGENTS.md conventions, theming cascade, design-decision system, generative type/instance pipeline, persistence mirror, and workspace/component patterns.
observations: Maintains a large table mapping every design concern to a global window registry (MS_TOKENS, MS_SURFACES, MS_STATUS, MS_SPACE, MS_RADIUS, MS_ELEVATION, MS_TYPE, MS_MOTION, MS_SHAPES, MS_REGISTRY, MS_TOAST, MS_DECISIONS, MS_KINDS, MS_RESOLVE, MS_ARTIFACTS, MS_STREAMS, MS_LAUNCH, MS_IDENTITY, MS_VI_BRAIN, MS_VI_CHAT, MS_PRODUCE, MS_MEMORY, etc.) and describes how each is used.
Central rule: never hardcode a value that a registry or token already owns; one-off hardcoded values are explicitly targeted for removal.
Loud-failure policy: unknown registry keys return visible sentinels (magenta #ff00ff, 0px) rather than silent fallbacks.
Design decisions in MS_DECISIONS do not own state; each maps to an existing tweak key, and committing an option is just setTweak(decision.tweak, option.value).

Generative system built on MS_KINDS universal types, MS_RESOLVE variable resolution (explicit > preference > memory > ai > default), MS_ARTIFACTS reactive/persistent instances, and MS_PRODUCE schema-guided content generation.
MS_VI_BRAIN and MS_VI_CHAT unify the floating Vi assistant and the identity console into one shared conversation and context, with action directives parsed and applied through real registries.
Persistence mirror in db/schema.sql maps kind, artifact, variable_binding, memory, preference, and design_value to the runtime registries.
Registry-driven extensibility contract: adding a kind to MS_KINDS automatically makes it selectable, configurable, producible, and interview-compatible without per-type wiring.
Components are inline-JSX attached to window; shared components exported via window.X = ...
Status colours are class-driven via Chip / chip--* in app.css, not inlined.
standouts: The single-source-of-truth registry table is the dominant organizing structure of the document.
The loud-failure sentinel design (#ff00ff) makes missing tokens visually obvious.
The generative type system (kinds + resolver + artifacts + produce + memory + schema.sql) is treated as the studio's core purpose.
MS_VI_BRAIN / MS_VI_CHAT collapse the floating assistant and identity console into one persistent conversation and action layer.
The registry-driven extensibility contract aims to make adding a new producible part zero-wiring.
conventions: Registry-centric tokenization: every colour, spacing step, radius, shadow, status, type size, motion value, and brand mark is referenced through a global window registry or CSS variable.
Per-folder AGENTS.md localization of conventions without contradiction.
Window-attached inline-JSX components exported via window.X in separate Babel script scopes.
CSS cascade order: colors_and_type.css -> app.css + per-surface CSS -> theme.css -> conformity.css -> responsive.css.
Class-driven status chips rather than inline status colours.
Resolution precedence: explicit > preference > memory > ai > default.
Design decisions map to existing tweak keys; no parallel stores.
Persistence schema mirrors runtime registry vocabulary.
concerns: MS_ARTIFACTS appears twice in the single-source-of-truth table with slightly different descriptions, which could confuse readers.
The 'Adding a workspace page' section is duplicated with different content.
The global window-registry pattern makes static dependency analysis and unit testing difficult from this file alone.
The 'never hardcode' rule is immediately followed by explicit exceptions, which may weaken enforcement unless the exceptions are mechanically validated.
Many registry descriptions are dense and overlapping, increasing the risk of inconsistent interpretation.
notes: This file is the high-level contract for an AI-extensible design system: it tells authors which registries to use and forbids hardcoding, but the actual API shapes and initialization order are only summarized.
questions: How are the window registries bootstrapped and ordered to avoid race conditions between scripts?
What exactly is window.claude — a mock, a bridge, or a real API — and how is it configured?
How does localStorage/Postgres persistence stay synchronized with the runtime registries?
How are per-folder AGENTS.md files discovered and validated against this canonical file?
What is the precise boundary between MS_VI_BRAIN (stateless composer) and MS_VI_CHAT (conversation state owner)?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document architecture and authoring conventions; the file fulfills that, but it also reads as a broad manifesto for a generative, AI-led design system.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth and token-driven design.
AI-led extensibility without per-change developer work.
Registry-driven generative design and learning loop.
Loud failure and visible debugging over silent fallbacks.
Unified, persistent AI conversation and identity layer.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a rigorous, registry-centric design system in which an AI assistant (Vi) can autonomously extend a React application by producing artifacts, learning project preferences, and preserving consistency through shared tokens and schemas.
novelty: The combination of a universal kind-based generative pipeline, context/memory-aware variable resolution, a unified AI conversation/action layer, and a runtime-to-SQL persistence mirror is unusual for a React prototype.
contribution: It contributes a comprehensive, opinionated convention document that defines the registry architecture, token-usage rules, generative artifact pipeline, and AI-authoring contract for the Counterpart Studio system.
summary_for_embedding: Counterpart Studio's CLAUDE.md is an architecture and authoring-conventions document that mandates registry-based single sources of truth, forbids hardcoded values, and describes a generative design system built on global window registries, a kind/instance resolver, AI-driven content production, and a mirrored persistence schema.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to instruct AI authors how to extend and maintain the Counterpart Studio prototype consistently through shared registries and a generative, learning design pipeline.
